title: Token-opt Slice C: Go-generated repo-map artifact (hard 1-2K token budget) injected into heavy phases — cut the code-exploration turns that dominate cache_read (turns × base)
summary: aider-style symbol/exports map ranked by relevance to the cycle's top_n / changed files, packed to a hard token budget, cached keyed by tree-SHA (paid once per change), injected into the heavy phases' prompts as the exploration starting point. UN-GATED 2026-07-17: telemetry now works (ArtifactPath fix) AND the base-cut slices (B-v1/v2/v3 clean-boot + --tools) already banked ~70% of the per-turn base (64K->19K), so turn-count is now the frontier. EVIDENCE: cache_read = turns x per-turn-base; heavy phases run 22-128 turns; a fault-localization transcript's Bash was 15/19 explore/search (grep/find/ls) vs 2 read + 2 git — i.e. ~80% of its Bash is hunting for code a repo-map would shortcut. HONEST SCOPING: realistic gain is MODEST (~15-20% turn reduction — the map shortens the FIND step, not the read/understand/iterate steps), MUCH smaller than the base-cut wins, and it carries QUALITY RISK: a stale/wrong/misleading map can send the agent to the wrong code and HURT the deliverable. So this is a real engineering slice (AST symbol extraction via go/parser + relevance ranking + tree-SHA cache + prompt-injection at phasecontract/render.go + per-phase config gate), NOT a config tweak, and MUST be validated over multiple cycles for BOTH turn-reduction AND unchanged deliverable quality (audit PASS rate) before broad enable. Start with fault-localization (the #1 explorer). Research: knowledge-base/research/token-concurrency-2026/ (oss.md 3, papers.md 5.3/3.3); scoped-context literature claims up to -82% but that is best-case whole-context, not turn-count.
acceptance: repo-map.md artifact generated at cycle start under a pinned token budget (test asserts budget)
cache keyed by tree-SHA (no regen on unchanged tree; test asserts cache hit)
map is FRESH per change (staleness cannot mislead — regenerated when tree-SHA changes)
injected only into gated heavy phases (config, phases_are_config_only)
SHADOW-VALIDATED: measured turn/cache_read reduction on the target phase AND audit PASS rate unchanged over >=5 cycles before enable
graceful degrade: a generation failure yields NO map (agent falls back to normal exploration), never a broken/partial map
files: go/internal/repomap (new pkg: go/parser AST symbol extraction + relevance ranking + tree-SHA cache)
connects_to: the token-telemetry ArtifactPath fix (measurement, now live)
the B-v1/v2/v3 clean-boot base cuts (banked ~70% per-turn base)
